content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


January 10, 2006


Carla Wardlow, Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Ste.620
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re:   Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-06-001

Dear Ms. Wardlow:

     This letter is in response to your request¹ for informal assistance regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") and the provisions of regulation 18329(c)(1) (copy enclosed).²

QUESTION

May political party committees combine the March 22 and March 30, 2006, pre-election statements for state and county central committees?

CONCLUSION

Political party committees may combine the March 22 and March 30, 2006, pre-election statements for state and county central committees and may file a single pre-election campaign statement on March 30 covering the period through March 25, 2006.




___________________________
¹ You submitted a revised request on January 9, 2005.

² Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations. Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(c)(3).)

File No. I-06-001
Page No. 2

ANALYSIS

Effective September 21, 2004, section 84200.5 of the Political Reform Act was
amended to require state and county political party committees to file pre-election
campaign statements in connection with a state election if the committee receives
contributions totaling $1,000 or more, or makes contributions or independent
expenditures totaling $500 or more during the period covered by the pre-election
statement. (Section 84200.5(f).)

A special state election will be held on April 11, 2006, to fill a vacancy in the 35th
Senate District, with a special runoff election schedule for June 6, 2006, the date of the
2006 statewide primary election. As a result, state and county political party committees
may be required to file a pre-election campaign statement on March 22 (covering the
period through March 17) in connection with the June 6 statewide primary election, and
another pre-election campaign statement on March 30 (covering the period March 18
through March 25) in connection with the April 11 election. The first pre-election
statement in connection with the April 11 election is due on March 2, covering the period
January 1 through February 25, 2006.

Section 84205 states:

The Commission may by regulation or written advice
permit candidates and committees to file campaign
statements combining statements and reports required to be
filed by this title.

Pursuant to section 84205, we advise that political party committees may file a
single pre-election campaign statement on March 30 covering the period through March
25, 2006.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
